creator: jeffrey_in_nyc
caption: 5 Fun and Cheap Things to do in NYC near Governor’s Island #nyc #NewYork #food #travel #visitnyc nybucketlist

transcription.text: Here are five fun and cheap things you must try in New York City near Governor's Island. You'll start your day by taking the ferry from lower Manhattan to the island while enjoying views of the New York City skyline. Once you arrive, you'll head over to Blazing Saddles for a free one-hour bike rental to cruise and sightsee around the island. You'll make your way over to Slide Hill where you can ride the longest outdoor slide in all of New York City. Then you can explore some of the old abandoned buildings including Castle Williams, Fort J, and you can even take a seat on an old abandoned rocking chair. You'll then visit Hemet Grove to lay back and take a nap on one of the swinging hammocks. You'll trek the hills where you can go up high and catch stunning views of the New York City skyline and the Statue of Liberty. And finally, you'll take the ferry back to lower Manhattan and cap off your evening with a beverage at the White Horse who's been serving up affordable drinks in good time since the 1930s.
